name: Biyo Dürüm
note: Kurdish fast-food spot in Paris, located near the Marche d'Aligre. The tiny canteen run by Serdar Kaya and his wife, Sedef, offers traditional Kurdish rolled durum sandwiches made with hand-rolled wafer-thin flatbreads and succulent skewers of veal, lamb, chicken, and minced kefte spit-roasted on charcoal.
caption: Biyo Dürüm | Kurdish fast-food spot in Paris, located near the Marche d'Aligre. The tiny canteen run by Serdar Kaya and his wife, Sedef, offers traditional Kurdish rolled durum sandwiches made with hand-rolled wafer-thin flatbreads and succulent skewers of veal, lamb, chicken, and minced kefte spit-roasted on charcoal.
